headline.text: L1 Sollich 2 enrober belt failed at 08:20, replaced and back running by *14:06* — full wire mesh swap and deep clean.

per_plant_summary.L1: Sollich 2 lost 6 hours to an enrober wire mesh failure — caramel and almonds fouled the belt, maintenance replaced it and the line restarted at 14:06. Sollich 3 ran caramel cluster with a brief capper jam overnight that cleared inside 20 minutes. Sollich 4 cooked Hawaiian Host caramel all day with no interruptions.
per_plant_summary.L2: ML3 saw blowoffs past the 2nd chicane all evening and stopped at 20:07 for maintenance to check belts — still working on it at shift end. FFS reported elevated bad product counts overnight from multiple shifts, then completed a level 2 clean and passed ATP for Monday. ML2 ran without incident.
per_plant_summary.L3: MF Matrix fought bag-sliding problems all day — Ray adjusted rails and pusher at 11:45 but accumulation continued into third shift. ML1 had a quick camera sensor fault at 04:07 (dirty sensor, cleaned, line running 10 minutes later). Truffle line and ML4 ran clean.

resolved.title: Enrober wire mesh belt failed and was replaced
resolved.summary: Wire mesh stopped turning at 08:20 due to caramel and almond buildup inside the enrober; maintenance replaced the entire belt, production cleaned and tempered, and the line restarted at 14:06.
resolved.resolution: New wire mesh installed and working by 11:33, line restarted at 14:06 after tempering — "The line has already started" (Victor Romero Sr.).

resolved.title: Capper jamming from overfeeding caused accumulation
resolved.summary: Accumulation built up overnight due to capper jams when overfed and lids not fully secured; line paused at 01:51 to clear and coach the team.
resolved.resolution: Cleared at 02:13 — "We're restarting the line now" (William Beattie) after coaching on jar feed rate and lid seating.

resolved.title: Internet outage during fiber hookup
resolved.summary: Plant-wide internet went down at 10:34 while facilities inspected and connected L2 to fiber.
resolved.resolution: Back online at 11:33 — "You all should have high speed connection now" (Chad Stone).

resolved.title: Sweco clamp nut threads stripped
resolved.summary: One of the sweco clamp nuts lost its threads at 03:14; maintenance attempted to rethread it.
resolved.resolution: Rethreaded and reinstalled at 05:47 — "Maintenance was able to get this rethreaded and it was put back on" (Gustavo Rodas).

resolved.title: Camera sensor fault stopped ML1 line
resolved.summary: Sensor fault at 04:07 triggered a line stop; maintenance and QA found a dirty sensor.
resolved.resolution: Cleaned and back running by 04:17 — "It was a dirty sensor. Line is up and running" (Imperial).

needs_attention.title: ML3 blowoffs past 2nd chicane, line stopped for belts
needs_attention.summary: Blowoffs started at 19:11, improved briefly, then continued; Brandon stopped the line at 20:07 suspecting belt movement and called maintenance.
needs_attention.status: Line down since 20:07 for maintenance to inspect belts; no restart confirmation by end of shift.

needs_attention.title: FFS seeing elevated bad product overnight
needs_attention.summary: Zack reported a spike in bad product at 02:07 from multiple shifts throughout the night; no root cause identified.
needs_attention.status: Line completed level 2 clean and passed ATP at 05:48; bad product issue not addressed before shutdown.

needs_attention.title: MF Matrix bags not sliding, pouches accumulating
needs_attention.summary: Bag texture causing persistent slide failures since 09:32; Ray adjusted side rails and pusher at 11:45 but Kimberly reported continued pouch accumulation at 23:56.
needs_attention.status: Still accumulating as of 23:56 on third shift despite earlier adjustments; Spanish note at 20:16 reports constant problems due to bag texture.
